task_completion.description: Open the Wazuh manager configuration file (ossec.conf).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like nano or vim) or the Wazuh dashboard configuration editor is open, showing the contents of the ossec.conf file.

task_completion.description: Define the firewall-drop command in the configuration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A <command> XML block is visible containing <name>firewall-drop</name> and <executable>firewall-drop</executable>.

task_completion.description: Create an active-response block that triggers the firewall-drop command.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An <active-response> XML block is visible containing <command>firewall-drop</command>.

task_completion.description: Set the active-response location to local.
Configure the active-response to trigger on rule ID 5763.
Set the active-response timeout to 600 seconds.

integrity.description: The agent is editing the actual ossec.conf file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file path shown in the editor is /var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf or the Wazuh web UI's official configuration section is being used, not a random text file.

integrity.description: The added configuration uses valid XML syntax.
integrity.visual_evidence: The <command> and <active-response> tags are properly opened and closed, and nested correctly within the parent <ossec_config> element.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply paste the task description into the file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file contains proper Wazuh XML configuration blocks, not plain English text from the prompt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target rule ID for SSH brute force is 5763. The target command to execute is 'firewall-drop'. The timeout must be set to 600 seconds. The active-response location should be set to 'local'.
